content.full_text:
September 28, 1990

Kathryn Donovan
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J St., 8th Floor
Sacramento CA 95814

Dear Ms Donovan:

A Slate Mailer Committee, Team for the '90s, will be sending out a slate mailing for the November 1990 election. In addition to the printed endorsements on the slate will be printed a "900" telephone number which voters may call to receive additional, verbal, endorsements. The additional endorsements will be for ballot items which did not pay to be listed on the mailer or to be endorsed on the phone.

The "900" phone projects operate as follows. A deposit is paid to the provider of the phone service to pay for programming and other setup charges and to cover any contracted minimum usage fee. When the service is in operation, customers who dial the "900" number will be billed by the telephone company for the calls based on rates printed on the slate mailer. Upon collection from the customers, the phone company will deduct a fee for its service and remit the balance to Team for the '90s, typically 80-90 days after the call has been placed.

Please give us some guidelines for reporting receipts from the phone company and for reporting endorsements made on the phone.

Sincerely,

David Bauer, treasurer
Team for the '90s
